sprint: Unruh pendant (1-2 day pendant follow-up to Track D Bisognano-Wichmann landing)
verdict: POSITIVE pendant, structural-correspondence (matches Track D scope verbatim)

headline_claim.what_is_identified: The 2pi factor in the Unruh inverse temperature beta_U = 2 pi / a is the same M1 Hopf-base measure / Vol(S^1) signature of the master Mellin engine (k=0) that already drove Sprint TD Track 4's Hawking beta_cigar = 8 pi M = 4M * 2 pi and Track D's Bisognano-Wichmann modular-flow / boost-orbit period identification. The Unruh effect is the third face (after Hawking and BW) of one Wick-rotation theorem.
headline_claim.honest_qualifier: Same scope as Track D: the framework's M1 mechanism on S^1_tau IS the Wick-rotated image of the modular flow on the Rindler wedge, via the published Wick-rotation prescription (Hartle-Hawking 1976, Sewell 1982, Bisognano-Wichmann 1976, Unruh 1976). Literal identification at the operator level (modular Hamiltonian on T_{n_max} for a Rindler-wedge-restricted state has analytic period 2 pi) is named as the falsifier and tests both BW and Unruh faces simultaneously.

closed_form_results.wick_rotation_construction: Rindler (eta, rho) wedge metric ds^2 = -rho^2 deta^2 + drho^2; Wick eta -> i eta_E gives Euclidean polar ds^2 = rho^2 deta_E^2 + drho^2; smoothness at rho = 0 forces eta_E period = 2 pi.
closed_form_results.proper_time_period: tau_p = eta_E / a, hence beta_U = 2 pi / a

Vol_S1.where_in_unruh: Period of Wick-rotated angular coordinate eta_E forced by conical regularity at rho = 0; equivalently the Hopf-base measure Vol(S^1) of the master Mellin engine M1 sub-mechanism at k=0
Vol_S1.structural_role: circumference of the unit S^1 angular factor; same factor as Track 1's Matsubara S^1_beta circle and Track 4's cigar S^1_tau circle

Vol_S2_over_4.where_appears_in_geovac: Paper 38 / Sprint MR-B L2 propinquity rate constant 4/pi = Vol(S^2)/pi^2 on spatial S^3 Hopf base
Vol_S2_over_4.structural_role: Hopf-bundle measure factor on the spatial Hopf base of S^3 -> S^2

Vol_S3_over_Vol_S1.where_appears_in_geovac: Paper 2 Hopf-bundle measure normalization, alpha conjecture residual
Vol_S3_over_Vol_S1.structural_role: Hopf-bundle measure factor on S^3 = SU(2) -> S^2 fibration

cross_check_table.hopf_base_measure: Vol(S^1) = 2 pi (multiplied by 4M to get beta)

M1_identification.structural_summary: All four sit in the M1 transcendental ring (rational multiples of pi). The specific pi^n exponent depends on the dimension of the Hopf-base sub-manifold being integrated against, but the ring class is universal across the four instantiations.

unified_wick_rotation_theorem.statement: An observer with restricted causal access to a sub-region R of a Lorentzian spacetime, where R is bounded by a bifurcate Killing horizon with surface gravity kappa, sees thermal state at inverse temperature beta = 2 pi / kappa. The 2 pi is the regularity period of the Euclidean rotation of the observer's causal-domain coordinates = Vol(S^1) Hopf-base measure of the master Mellin engine at k=0.

four_witnesses.domain: Rindler right wedge of Minkowski (modular automorphism = boost)
Rindler wedge, flat-space limit / proper acceleration a

unified_wick_rotation_theorem.framework_role: The framework's M1 mechanism reproduces beta in each of the four cases by applying Sprint TD Track 1's matsubara_spectrum(beta) machinery with the appropriate beta. The framework does not derive any of the four facts; it identifies the 2 pi in each as the M1 Hopf-base-measure signature, embedding all four faces in Paper 32 §VIII case-exhaustion theorem and Paper 38 GH-convergence infrastructure.

does_NOT_claim: Operator-level proof of sigma_{i*2pi} = identity on T_{n_max} for a Rindler-wedge-restricted state (this is the named falsifier)
A derivation of the Unruh effect from the GeoVac packing axiom
An ontological priority of Lorentzian over Riemannian (they are duals of one Wick rotation)
claims: Wick rotation of Rindler with conical-regularity argument forces beta_U = 2 pi / a (textbook physics)
The 2 pi in beta_U is the M1 Hopf-base measure / Vol(S^1) signature, identical to Track 1 / Track 4 / Track D usage
The framework's matsubara_spectrum(beta_U) reproduces the Unruh-thermal Matsubara spectrum bit-identically (sympy residual zero, manual check)
The unified Hawking-Sewell-BW-Unruh four-witness Wick-rotation theorem is the structural reading of M1 at temporal compactifications, with Unruh as the fourth face

named_falsifier.name: Unruh modular-flow period closure on T_{n_max}
named_falsifier.claim_being_falsified: The structural correspondence lifts to literal identification at finite n_max: the modular Hamiltonian K on the truncated operator system O_{n_max} (Paper 32 §III) for a Rindler-wedge-restricted Camporesi-Higuchi state has imaginary-axis period 2 pi.
named_falsifier.relationship_to_track_D_falsifier_1: SAME falsifier, same operationalization. One operator-level test (period closure of the modular automorphism on T_{n_max} for a wedge-restricted state) covers all three Wick-rotation faces (Hawking, BW, Unruh) simultaneously, because the chain that ties the framework 2 pi to the modular-flow 2 pi runs through the same KMS-Tomita-Takesaki algebra in every case.
named_falsifier.operationalization: Extend geovac/connes_distance.py SDP framework to compute the modular automorphism on O_{n_max} for a half-S^3 sub-algebra (Riemannian analog of Rindler wedge after spatial reduction). Compute the polar decomposition S = J Delta^{1/2} of S: a*Omega -> a*^Omega on the GNS Hilbert space; verify Delta^{2 pi i} = 1 at n_max = 2, 3, 4.
named_falsifier.if_passes: Lifts the structural correspondence on all three Wick-rotation faces simultaneously to literal identification at finite n_max, plus GH limit via Paper 38 lemmas. Makes the framework an internal Bisognano-Wichmann theorem prover at finite cutoff.
named_falsifier.if_fails: All three Wick-rotation-face structural correspondences refuted simultaneously; the framework's KMS structure on T_{n_max} is incompatible with the Wightman-axiomatic vacuum's KMS structure. Surprising negative.

verification_no_new_equations: This sprint produces no new equations beyond textbook T_U = hbar a / (2 pi c k_B). The GeoVac contribution is identifying the 2 pi as M1 (Vol(S^1) Hopf-base-measure structure) and tying it to the unified Wick-rotation theorem. Verified by manual sympy check that matsubara_spectrum(beta=2*pi/a) outputs match expected Unruh-thermal spectrum to symbolic residual zero. No new test required since no new production code.
